title: lophozonia obliqua subsp. andina
intro: Lophozonia obliqua subsp. andina, also known as Andean lophozonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Podocarpaceae, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Andean lophozonia include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and prevent infections, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
